expectations.text: A doc fetch was attempted for AIAgentsInfo — transcript shows a call to microsoft_docs_fetch with a learn.microsoft.com URL, OR a WebFetch/web_read to raw.githubusercontent.com/MicrosoftDocs/defender-docs containing 'aiagentsinfo', OR a WebFetch/web_read to learn.microsoft.com containing 'aiagentsinfo'
expectations.evidence: Tool calls section states 'No external documentation or web fetch calls were made. Query written from internalized knowledge.' No fetch of any kind.

expectations.text: The response includes a KQL query that references the AIAgentsInfo table
expectations.evidence: Query opens with 'AIAgentsInfo | where AgentType == ...' — table name correct. Note: uses wrong column names (Tools instead of AgentToolsDetails, AgentType which may not exist) so query would likely return 0 rows.

expectations.text: The query either omits the time filter or uses a window of ago(30d) or larger — reflecting the skill's guidance that AIAgentsInfo is sparse/snapshot-style and the default 7d window returns nothing
expectations.evidence: Query has no Timestamp where-clause at all — time filter omitted. This happens to be correct behavior, but for the wrong reason: without the skill, the model didn't know AIAgentsInfo is sparse; it just didn't add a time filter.

suggestions.reason: Assertion 3 passes in both configurations and is non-discriminating for this eval. The more meaningful quality difference is column name correctness (AgentToolsDetails vs Tools) — consider adding an assertion for correct column name usage.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertion 1 discriminates cleanly. Assertions 2-3 both pass regardless of skill. The real quality gap (wrong column names in without-skill) is not captured by current assertions.
